title: Latent Safety-Car / VSC Detector From Field-Wide Pace Shock
summary: Pit timing changes sharply under neutralization because pit-loss economics change, but the dataset has no explicit SC/VSC flag.
rationale: Pit timing changes sharply under neutralization because pit-loss economics change, but the dataset has no explicit SC/VSC flag. Motorsport simulation papers and F1 strategy analysis both emphasize yellow-flag windows as pivotal. A weakly supervised latent flag proxy built from synchronized field-wide lap-time shocks could recover part of this missing state without external joins.
implementation_hint: Per `Race_Year x LapNumber`, compute robust field aggregates such as median/trimmed mean of `LapTime_Delta`, dispersion, fraction with extreme positive deltas, and abrupt changes versus previous lap. Convert these into a `NeutralizationProb` or binary latent state, then interact it with `LapsRemaining_Est`, `TyreLife`, `Compound`, and `PitStop` history; optionally downweight anomalous laps in main-model training.
expected_effect: Could lift generalization on races where the current model misses abrupt strategic regime shifts.
risk: Field shocks can also come from weather or local incidents, so the latent label will be noisy and may hurt if treated too confidently.
